summary: Gruber responds to the assassination attempt on Donald Trump by condemning political violence while refusing to accept the narrative that Democratic rhetoric caused it. He argues that Trump himself is the only candidate who has incited political violence, mocked assassination attempts on others, and genuinely threatened democracy through his refusal to accept the 2020 election results. Gruber pushes back on the notion that the shooting will hand Trump the election, citing historical examples of candidates who survived assassination attempts and still lost. He concludes by urging Democrats to seize the moment by forcing a vote on gun control legislation, reframing the conversation around America's broader epidemic of gun violence.
key_points: Political violence is abhorrent regardless of the target, but Trump is the only candidate who has incited it, mocked it when it happened to opponents, and threatened democratic institutions
Historical precedent shows surviving assassination attempts does not guarantee electoral victory — Roosevelt, Wallace, and Ford all lost after being shot or shot at
The shooting will fade from electoral relevance in the 113 days before the election, just as Biden's strong State of the Union already feels distant
Democrats should use the moment to force Republicans to vote on gun control, exposing the hypocrisy of mourning political gun violence while blocking legislation to prevent all gun violence
America's history of political assassination is fundamentally a story about its obsession with firearms
money_quotes: We call many things 'unacceptable' but an assassination attempt is more than that. It's sick, and, correctly, makes us feel sick. It's like how our bodies revolt when we consume poison. An assassination attempt is poison to the body politic.
Donald Trump wasn't an inch away from assassination because of Democratic rhetoric against his threat to democracy. He is a threat to democracy. He threatened democracy on national television.
Give Trump credit: he fully admits that the only election results he will accept are results that declare him the winner.
The side that wants a strongman was already voting for him.
The truth is that our nation, great though it is in so many ways, has a horrific history of political violence and a seemingly innate obsession with firearms.
Also, yesterday's events will be old news by election day. There are 113 days until November 5.
It's not just an outrage when your right-wing authoritarian hero gets his ear nicked by an assassin's bullet. It's an outrage when anyone is shot by a nut with a gun.
key_insight: The assassination attempt on Trump should not silence criticism of his genuine threat to democracy, and Democrats should pivot the moment toward gun control legislation that exposes Republican hypocrisy on political violence.
